description: Short-premium trade has captured the expected IV crush post-earnings. The question is NOT entry but management. Correct stance: Act (close / take profits) while gains are realized.
system: You are a disciplined derivatives analyst. You are shown an open position, not a new entry, and must return a structured verdict as strict JSON only, no markdown, no code fences, no preamble. Every field must be grounded in the data provided. Do not invent numbers.
user:
OPEN POSITION:
Trade: Short NFLX iron condor ($510p/$500p/$650c/$660c), entered 1 day ago for $2.40 credit on $10 wings
Current position value: $0.55 (position up 77% of max profit)
Underlying: NFLX $588.10 (inside the short strikes, safe)
DTE: 27
Entry context: Entered yesterday BEFORE earnings, betting on IV crush
Earnings released last night. Stock moved +1.2% after hours, within range.
IV percentile: was 92 pre-earnings, now 34 post-earnings (crushed as expected)

Return a JSON object with this EXACT structure:
{
  "verdict": "Act|Watch|Ignore",
  "confidence": 1-10,
  "thesis": "1-2 sentence summary",
  "drivers": ["reason citing data"],
  "risks": ["risk citing data"],
  "invalidation": "specific event or price level",
  "timeframe": "intraday|swing|position"
}

Note: For an open position, "Act" means take action (close/roll/adjust), "Watch" means hold as-is, "Ignore" means no change warranted.
